title: Logger field or property name should follow a naming convention
description: Logger field or property name should comply with a naming conventionAnalyze your codeconsistency-conventionalmaintainabilityCode SmellloggingWhy is this an issue?How can I fix it?More InfoSharing some naming conventions is a key point to make it possible for a team to efficiently collaborate. This rule checks that the logger field or property name matches a provided regular expression.The rule supports the most popular logging frameworks:Nuget package -Microsoft.Extensions.LoggingNuget package -SerilogNuget package -Castle.CoreNuget package -NLogNuget package -log4netAvailable In:Catch issues on the fly,in your IDEDetect issues in your GitHub, Azure DevOps Services, Bitbucket Cloud, GitLab repositoriesAnalyze code in youron-premise CI©2025SonarSource Sàrl. All rights reserved.Privacy Polic
recommendation: Review and fix according to the rule guidelines.

triggering_conditions: ILogger field name not in allowed list (logger, _logger, LOG)
exclusion_conditions: ILogger field name is logger, _logger, or LOG
checks.reasoning: Logger names should follow naming conventions for consistency and team collaboration
